advisory.location: behavioral-spec.md:337-338 (DOC-EVID-002a top-up paragraph, step 4), verification-architecture.md:227-229 (PROP-020 step 4)
advisory.finding: Unchanged from iteration-4's ADV-004-TOPUP-BUDGET-ARITHMETIC-LOOSE: top-up-confirmation preflight retries are described as 'up to 3 retries with a 2-minute wait between attempts (10-minute total budget)' -- 3 attempts with 2-minute gaps is 4 minutes of pure waiting, not 10, unless read charitably as a ceiling with headroom for command/RPC latency per attempt. Not touched by the it-4-codex revision (out of this iteration's diff scope), still non-blocking, still just a documentation-precision nit.

Unchanged from iteration-3/4's carried advisory: the top-up send itself is still described only as 'the ORCHESTRATOR ... performs it' with no specific tool/command named for the on-chain transfer, since blockrun-cli's own wallet action set has no send/transfer action (src/args/wallet.ts, confirmed no send action exists). Grounded by direct precedent (VERIFICATION.md's funding tx from the same 0x810f... source to the same sandbox address) and out of this iteration's diff scope. Non-blocking, unchanged risk profile.

resolvedFromIteration4Codex: FIND-004-001-LEDGER-FALLBACK-NOT-CONSERVATIVE -- VERIFIED RESOLVED. The ledger-derived-floor branch (old step 3: 'derive a CONSERVATIVE lower-bound balance from cli-budget.json's global.spent subtracted from the $0.59 funding amount... MAY be used ONLY to decide whether to attempt the NEXT cheapest step') has been fully REMOVED from both behavioral-spec.md (DOC-EVID-002a) and verification-architecture.md (PROP-020 step 2). The resolution order is now explicitly capped at exactly TWO paths to an actionable balance -- (1) retry the live preflight up to 3x, (2) the on-chain eth_call fallback -- with a hard STOP if both fail. Both spec files now state, near-verbatim in each: ledger/global.spent + the $0.59 funding figure 'MAY be written to the evidence output as a REFERENCE/cross-check figure ONLY... and SHALL NEVER be used to justify proceeding with a paid re-run, a top-up decision, or ANY spend when both step 1 and step 2 have failed.' I independently recomputed the arithmetic codex flagged: $0.59 - $0.316001 (real cli-budget.json global.spent, confirmed by reading /Users/anicca/blockrun-cli-e2e-home/.blockrun/cli-budget.json directly: {"global":{"spent":0.31600100000000003,...}}) = $0.273999, which is indeed higher than VERIFICATION.md's real recorded end balance $0.264748 (confirmed by reading VERIFICATION.md:18 directly) -- the spec now cites this exact math only as the historical justification for WHY the floor was removed, never as a live decision path. Scanned both files for any orphaned reference to 'lower bound' / 'conservative' outside this explanatory context (grep -n -i 'lower.bound|conservative' across both specs) -- all 5 remaining hits are explanatory-only, none authorize a spend. The top-up shortfall formula and PROP-020's final assertion both now say 'resolved balance (live preflight number OR the on-chain fallback number -- NEVER a ledger-derived estimate)'. No remaining path where an estimated/derived value can authorize spend.

FIND-004-002-ETH-CALL-FALLBACK-PAYLOAD-UNDERSPECIFIED -- VERIFIED RESOLVED. The previous placeholder ('<Base-USDC balanceOf(...) call payload>') is replaced in both spec files with a fully concrete, executable invocation. I ground-truthed all four transcribed claims directly against /Users/anicca/blockrun-cli/src/shell/wallet.ts (read in full, lines 1-260): (a) USDC_ADDRESS = '0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913' at wallet.ts:25 -- exact match; (b) calldata construction `0x70a08231000000000000000000000000${address.slice(2)}` at wallet.ts:238 -- exact match, and the spec's fully-instantiated example for the sandbox address (0x70a08231 + 24 zero hex chars + a5cef4943c3f8f34e5138b5bcde6b88746a5c804, lowercased/0x-stripped) correctly reproduces the ABI left-zero-pad-to-32-bytes encoding of a 20-byte address; (c) decode logic `Number(BigInt(raw)) / 1e6` in parseBaseUsdcCallResult() at wallet.ts:229-232 -- exact match, function spans exactly those 4 lines; (d) getBaseUsdcBalance()'s 3-free-RPC-then-null behavior cited at wallet.ts:234-255 -- exact match (function spans exactly those lines, tries mainnet.base.org / base.llamarpc.com / 1rpc.io/base per wallet.ts:26-30). I also verified the RPC_PRICE_USD=$0.002 citation at src/args/rpc.ts:9 (exact line) and the image/video/music txHash asymmetry claim reused elsewhere in these sections (image.ts:83-84 has no txHash field; video.ts:71/music.ts:55 conditionally include txHash from manual-x402.ts's X-Payment-Receipt header) -- both exact matches by direct read/grep. The fallback is now mechanically executable as written with zero remaining templated/invented fields.

mirrorConsistencyCheck.detail: Compared the revised DOC-EVID-002/002a (behavioral-spec.md) against the revised PROP-020 (verification-architecture.md) clause-by-clause: contract address, selector, calldata construction, decode logic, source line citations, RPC_PRICE_USD citation, retry counts (3x/30s for preflight, 3x/2min/10min-budget for top-up confirmation), the 'no prior-balance gate needed, unconditional attempt is safe per REQ-220/REQ-221' justification, and the 'ledger data is reference-only, never spend-authorizing' rule all appear near-verbatim in both files with no contradiction. This also closes the iteration-4 codex advisory ADV-004-002-PROP-BEHAVIORAL-FALLBACK-DRIFT (behavioral-spec previously had a balance-covers-$0.002 gate that PROP-020 omitted) -- both files now consistently say the fallback attempt is UNCONDITIONAL/ungated, so that drift no longer exists (not re-flagging as it was already non-blocking and is now moot). Budget guard section (verification-architecture.md §3) headroom recomputation (≈$0.231 worst case incl. up to 3x$0.002 fallback contingency vs ≈$0.2647 last-observed balance ≈ $0.034 headroom) is internally consistent with PROP-020's per-step fallback description (one fallback attempt possible per media step, cheapest-first, max 3 total).

newBlockingScan.scope: Full diff of commit 3bc67d7 (behavioral-spec.md +89/-26 lines around DOC-EVID-002/002a; verification-architecture.md +90/-25 lines around PROP-020 step 1/2 and §3 budget guard) plus a targeted grep sweep of both files for orphaned references to the removed ledger-floor mechanism.

note: Spec re-review iteration 5 (final), fresh adversary instance, zero Builder context. Both codex iteration-4 blocking findings (FIND-004-001 ledger-floor-not-conservative, FIND-004-002 eth_call-payload-underspecified) are verified resolved by direct, independent inspection of src/shell/wallet.ts, src/args/rpc.ts, src/commands/{image,video,music}.ts, the real /Users/anicca/blockrun-cli-e2e-home/.blockrun/cli-budget.json file, and VERIFICATION.md -- not by trusting the spec's own citations. No new blocking contradictions introduced by the revision. The two carried-over advisories (top-up confirmation budget arithmetic, unnamed top-up send tool) remain open but non-blocking and were not touched by this revision's diff scope; not re-litigating them per instructions. Design choices already accepted in prior iterations (lean mode, docs-only scope, top-up-from-0x810f funding policy, image's cost_usd-based settlement proof, DOC-* count of 44) were not re-litigated. Verdict: PASS on both dimensions -- this spec is ready to proceed to Phase 2a (TDD/RED).
